title: Transformation of E. coli  with  plasmid
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bl32p","text":"This protocol can be used to transform electrocompetent E. coli cells with a plasmid.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":56,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Wash cuvette with 100 % (v/v) ethanol and UV in the hood for 00:20:00
Pre-thaw electrocompetent cells and plasmid on wet ice for 00:10:00
Add 1 µL of plasmid DNA to 50 µL electrocompetent cells
Transfer cell-DNA mixture into the cuvette and add equal amount ( 50 µL ) of MilliQ
Let the cuvette sit in wet ice for 00:20:00
Place cuvette in BioRad pulser and pulse at 2.5 keV
Within 00:00:40 add 100 µL of 2X LB to the cuvette
Place cuvette in an incubator at 37 °C for 00:30:00
Then use a bent pipette tip to spread the cuvette's contents onto a plate made with LB agar and the appropriate antibiotics. Spread until plate is dry
Place the plate in an incubator at 37 °C Overnight
Make glycerol stocks of transformed cells and store for future use
creator: sanjana
